operating_state: Worldwide revenue increased 5.8% to $23.7 billion in Q2 2025, driven by U.S. growth outpacing international markets. Innovative Medicine grew with oncology achieving 24% growth led by DARZALEX and CARVYKTI expansion, while immunology declined 15.4% primarily due to STELARA biosimilar competition. MedTech expanded 7.3% led by cardiovascular devices including SHOCKWAVE acquisition contribution, while orthopaedics declined due to restructuring disruption. Neuroscience grew 15.1% with CAPLYTA contributing following the Intra-Cellular acquisition.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 67.9% from 69.4% in the prior year quarter, reflecting higher cost of products sold driven by unfavorable product mix from STELARA decline and increased intangible asset amortization from recent acquisitions. Selling and administrative expenses declined as a percentage of sales to 24.8% due to corporate administrative expense rationalization, while research and development remained stable at 14.8% of sales.
material_events: Completed $14.5 billion acquisition of Intra-Cellular Therapies on April 2, 2025, funded through senior unsecured notes issuance, adding CAPLYTA for bipolar depression and schizophrenia treatment. Reversed approximately $7.0 billion from talc litigation reserves during the six-month period following Texas Bankruptcy Court dismissal of Red River case in March 2025. Initiated Surgery franchise restructuring program and continued Orthopaedics restructuring. Board declared quarterly dividends of $1.30 per share representing a 4.8% increase.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation resulted in negative impact with international operations facing ongoing headwinds. STELARA faces continued biosimilar competition in Europe and United States with launches expected throughout 2025 following patent settlement agreements. Medicare Part D redesign under Inflation Reduction Act shifts greater cost burden to manufacturers impacting multiple product lines, while China volume-based procurement negatively affects Surgery and Orthopaedics franchises.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued growth in oncology portfolio driven by DARZALEX and newer launches including CARVYKTI and RYBREVANT, with integration of Intra-Cellular acquisition contributing to neuroscience growth trajectory. Surgery and Orthopaedics franchise restructuring programs expected to complete over next two years. Company maintains focus on external innovation through strategic acquisitions and partnerships while pursuing comprehensive talc litigation resolution through tort system with bellwether trials scheduled for November 2025.
uncertainty_profile: New U.S. tax legislation enacted July 2025 will increase foreign earnings tax rate from 10.5% to 12.6% effective fiscal 2026, with estimated one-time deferred tax remeasurement cost of approximately $1.0 billion in Q3 2025. Talc litigation exposure remains significant with approximately 70,000 pending plaintiffs and $4.0 billion current reserve following bankruptcy dismissal. Multiple patent infringement litigations ongoing including CAPLYTA cases with generic manufacturers and ongoing litigation across major revenue contributors facing generic competition threats.
